title: The Draft Riots convulse the city
note: Scorsese's epic of Five Points gang warfare climaxes with the 1863 Civil War draft riots tearing through Lower Manhattan.

title: Famine Irish transform the city
note: The film's Five Points slum is populated by the famine-driven Irish immigrants and the nativist backlash they provoked at midcentury.

title: Washington inaugurated at Federal Hall
note: Miranda's hip-hop retelling of Alexander Hamilton's rise dramatizes the founding-era New York where Washington took the first presidential oath and Hamilton built the young republic's finances.

title: The Bank of New York is founded
note: The musical foregrounds Hamilton as the architect of American finance, the same figure who founded the Bank of New York in 1784.

title: Metropolitan Opera House opens on Broadway
note: Wharton's novel dissects 1870s Gilded Age high society, the same insular opera-box world of old New York money that built the new Metropolitan Opera.

title: Metropolitan Opera House opens on Broadway
note: Fellowes's series set in 1880s New York centers on the clash between old money and the newly rich who bankrolled institutions like the Metropolitan Opera.

title: Riis publishes How the Other Half Lives
note: Carr's 1896-set thriller, with Theodore Roosevelt as police commissioner, plunges into the same Lower East Side tenement underworld Riis had exposed a few years earlier.

title: Stanford White murdered at Madison Square Garden
note: Doctorow weaves the real Evelyn Nesbit and the 1906 killing of architect Stanford White into his panorama of turn-of-the-century New York.

title: Williamsburg Bridge opens over the East River
note: Smith's semi-autobiographical novel follows a poor immigrant family in early-1900s Williamsburg, the Brooklyn neighborhood the new bridge was rapidly filling with newcomers.

title: Ellis Island immigration hits its peak
note: The film's flashbacks open with young Vito Corleone arriving alone through Ellis Island in 1901 and building a life in Little Italy.

title: Prohibition ushers in the speakeasy era
note: Fitzgerald's Jazz Age novel of bootlegged wealth and lavish parties captures the New York of 1922, at the height of Prohibition.

title: Prohibition ushers in the speakeasy era
work: Once Upon a Time in America
note: Leone's epic traces Jewish gangsters on the Lower East Side from their youth through the bootlegging years of late Prohibition.

title: The Cross Bronx Expressway is completed
note: Norton reset Jonathan Lethem's novel in 1950s New York, adding a power broker modeled on Robert Moses whose expressways and clearances drive the plot.

title: The Puerto Rican Great Migration peaks
note: The musical's Jets-and-Sharks conflict dramatizes tensions around Puerto Rican migration in a San Juan Hill soon cleared for Lincoln Center.

title: The blackout that broke the city's spirit
note: Lee's film set in a Bronx neighborhood during the summer of 1977 uses the citywide blackout and Son of Sam killings as its backdrop.

title: The AIDS epidemic surfaces in New York
note: Kushner's two-part epic follows New Yorkers in 1985 as the AIDS crisis reshapes their lives, the defining dramatic work about the epidemic.

title: The AIDS epidemic surfaces in New York
note: Larson's rock musical follows young East Village artists living under the shadow of HIV/AIDS in early-1990s Lower Manhattan.

title: Times Square is reborn
note: The series chronicles the sex trade and porn industry of a seedy 1970s and 1980s Times Square, the gritty era swept away by the district's later rebirth.

title: The crack epidemic engulfs the city
note: Lee's film unfolds over one sweltering day of racial tension in a Bedford-Stuyvesant, Brooklyn, gripped by the pressures of the late-1980s city.
